faq.question: What time is it in Cairo right now?
faq.answer: Cairo operates on Eastern European Time (EET/UTC+2) year-round. Egypt permanently adopted UTC+2 in 2015 after discontinuing daylight saving time. Cairo is 7 hours ahead of New York (EST), 2 hours ahead of London (GMT), and 6 hours behind Tokyo. This consistent timezone makes scheduling with Cairo straightforward as there are no seasonal clock changes.

faq.question: What time zone is Cairo in?
faq.answer: Cairo uses Eastern European Time (EET), which is UTC+2 throughout the entire year. Since Egypt discontinued DST in 2015, Cairo maintains a permanent UTC+2 offset without seasonal adjustments. This timezone is shared with cities like Johannesburg, Athens, Helsinki, and Jerusalem, making regional coordination efficient across North Africa, the Middle East, and Eastern Europe.

faq.question: Does Cairo observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Egypt permanently discontinued daylight saving time in 2015 after several years of starting and stopping the practice. Cairo now maintains Eastern European Time (UTC+2) year-round, providing consistency for business operations, tourism, and international coordination. This decision followed concerns about energy savings effectiveness and public confusion from frequent DST policy changes.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Cairo and New York?
faq.answer: Cairo is 7 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time (November-March) and 6 hours ahead during Eastern Daylight Time (March-November). When it's 9 AM in New York, it's 4 PM in Cairo (EST) or 3 PM (EDT). This difference impacts business communications between Egypt and the US East Coast, with optimal overlap windows in the late Cairo afternoon.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Cairo and London?
faq.answer: Cairo is 2 hours ahead of London during Greenwich Mean Time (October-March) and 1 hour ahead during British Summer Time (March-October). When it's noon in London, it's 2 PM in Cairo (GMT) or 1 PM (BST). The moderate time difference facilitates strong business connections between Egypt and the UK, with excellent working hours overlap throughout the day.

faq.question: What time does the stock exchange open in Cairo?
faq.answer: The Egyptian Exchange (EGX) operates Sunday through Thursday from 10:00 AM to 2:30 PM EET (UTC+2), with a midday break from 12:30 PM to 1:00 PM. Trading does not occur on Fridays and Saturdays, aligning with Egypt's weekend. Pre-opening sessions begin at 9:30 AM, and the exchange follows the Islamic calendar for holidays, which affects trading schedules.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Cairo from the US?
faq.answer: The optimal time to call Cairo from the US East Coast is between 7 AM and 11 AM EST (2 PM - 6 PM Cairo time), catching Cairo business hours before their workday ends. From the West Coast, call between 4 AM and 8 AM PST (2 PM - 6 PM Cairo time). Avoid calling after 11 AM EST/8 AM PST, as you'll reach Cairo after typical business hours.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Cairo?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Cairo run Sunday through Thursday from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM EET, with many offices closing early on Thursdays around 2-3 PM. Government offices typically operate 8:00 AM to 3:00 PM. The Egyptian weekend is Friday-Saturday, with Friday being the primary day of rest. During Ramadan, business hours shift significantly, often operating 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM with modified schedules.

content_blocks.title: Cairo Time Zone: Eastern European Time (EET)
content_blocks.content: Cairo operates on Eastern European Time (EET/UTC+2) throughout the entire year, maintaining one of Africa's most consistent timezone standards. Egypt's decision to permanently adopt UTC+2 in 2015 ended decades of irregular daylight saving time policies that created confusion for businesses and travelers. This permanent timezone places Cairo 2 hours ahead of Coordinated Universal Time, aligning it with major regional hubs like Johannesburg, Athens, and Jerusalem. The stability of Cairo's timezone makes it an attractive destination for international business operations in the Middle East and North Africa region. Unlike many European and North American cities that shift their clocks twice yearly, Cairo's consistent time simplifies long-term scheduling and reduces the complexity of coordinating meetings across multiple continents. For travelers and business professionals managing communications between Cairo and cities that do observe DST, it's essential to note that time differences will vary by one hour depending on the season. Compare Cairo's current time with New York, London, or Dubai to coordinate your international schedule effectively.

content_blocks.title: Business Hours and Working Culture in Cairo
content_blocks.content: Cairo follows a Sunday-through-Thursday work week, reflecting Egypt's Islamic cultural heritage where Friday serves as the primary day of worship and rest. Standard business hours typically run from 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM EET, though many private sector companies operate from 8:30 AM to 4:30 PM. Government ministries and public sector offices generally maintain earlier hours, opening at 8:00 AM and closing by 3:00 PM. The Egyptian Exchange (EGX), one of Africa's oldest stock markets, operates from 10:00 AM to 2:30 PM with a midday break from 12:30 PM to 1:00 PM, creating a compressed trading window compared to Western exchanges. International businesses scheduling meetings with Cairo-based partners should target the window between 10:00 AM and 3:00 PM EET for optimal availability. This translates to 3:00 AM - 8:00 AM EST for US East Coast companies or 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM GMT for European partners. During the holy month of Ramadan, business operations shift dramatically with shortened hours (typically 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM) and reduced productivity during fasting hours. Cairo's timezone provides excellent overlap with European business hours, making it a strategic location for companies serving both Middle Eastern and European markets. Use our Meeting Planner to find the perfect time for your Cairo business calls, or check the time difference with Paris for European coordination.

content_blocks.title: International Calling and Communication with Cairo
content_blocks.content: Successfully timing calls to Cairo from North America requires careful consideration of the 6-7 hour time difference with the US East Coast and 9-10 hour difference with the West Coast. The optimal calling window from New York is between 7:00 AM and 11:00 AM EST (2:00 PM - 6:00 PM Cairo time), catching Cairo professionals in their afternoon working hours before the evening commute begins. West Coast callers should plan for early morning calls between 4:00 AM and 8:00 AM PST to reach the same Cairo business hours. European businesses enjoy a much more favorable timezone relationship, with London-based companies able to call Cairo comfortably between 10:00 AM and 3:00 PM GMT, reaching Cairo between noon and 5:00 PM local time. The 2-hour (or 1-hour during BST) difference creates substantial working hours overlap that facilitates real-time collaboration. For Asia-Pacific regions, Cairo sits 5 hours behind Singapore and 7 hours behind Tokyo, making afternoon Cairo hours align with early morning Asia time. When scheduling recurring international calls with Cairo, remember that Egypt does not observe daylight saving time, so time differences with DST-observing countries will shift by one hour twice per year. This consistency is actually advantageous for maintaining stable meeting schedules—once you establish a good meeting time, it remains constant from Cairo's perspective. Verify current time differences using our comparison tools for Cairo to London, Cairo to Tokyo, or Cairo to Los Angeles before scheduling important calls.
